root_cause.text: The `MercurialStatus` class inappropriately extended `AbstractModelObject` while being an `UnprotectedRootAction`, inadvertently exposing the search functionality (and thus sensitive data like nodes and users) to unauthenticated users.
root_cause.evidence: The patch removes `extends AbstractModelObject` from the `MercurialStatus` class signature and deletes the `getSearchUrl()` method, disabling the exposed search interface.

vuln_statements.content: public class MercurialStatus extends AbstractModelObject implements UnprotectedRootAction {

intent_record.how: Cleans up dependencies without changing program behavior.

intent_record.intent_summary: Remove AbstractModelObject inheritance from MercurialStatus to simplify the class structure.
intent_record.what: Removed the inheritance of AbstractModelObject from the MercurialStatus class.
intent_record.how: The class now solely implements UnprotectedRootAction, dropping unnecessary inherited methods and properties from AbstractModelObject.
Removes an overridden method, likely to rely on the parent class implementation.

grouping_result.intent: Remove AbstractModelObject inheritance from MercurialStatus to simplify the class structure.

consistency_review.core_intent: Remove AbstractModelObject inheritance and its unused import from MercurialStatus.

relevance_review.reason: The group directly addresses the vulnerability by removing the AbstractModelObject inheritance from MercurialStatus, which was the root cause of the unauthorized search functionality exposure.
Removing the getSearchUrl method is directly tied to mitigating the unintended exposure of search functionality described in the root cause.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
Added URL-based build triggering, similar to the git plugin.

Visiting <root>/mercurial/notifyCommit?url=<repo_url> will
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit df5d889792a9ed9af8f42b4a8142bf0a2224058c
Author: Kamil Kisiel <kamil@zymeworks.com>
Date:   Fri Dec 2 15:01:22 2011 -0800

    Added URL-based build triggering, similar to the git plugin.
    
    Visiting <root>/mercurial/notifyCommit?url=<repo_url> will trigger all
    builds that reference that repository URL.

diff --git a/src/main/java/hudson/plugins/mercurial/MercurialStatus.java b/src/main/java/hudson/plugins/mercurial/MercurialStatus.java
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..385b60
bic_trace_step.reason: The commit introduces the `MercurialStatus` class with the vulnerable signature `public class MercurialStatus extends AbstractModelObject implements UnprotectedRootAction`, which is exactly the root cause of the vulnerability described.
